entry.title: Re: RV_MESSAGES_GET inside MM07MFB9_BUCHEN_AUSFUEHREN and...
entry.content:
Hello @Shizofrenik 

That is how RV_MESSAGES_GET is implemented. It initializes both TAB_XNAST and TAB_YNAST at the very beginning:

FUNCTION RV_MESSAGES_GET.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Lokale Schnittstelle:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(MSG_KAPPL) LIKE  NAST-KAPPL DEFAULT SPACE
*"     VALUE(MSG_OBJKY_FROM) LIKE  NAST-OBJKY DEFAULT ' '
*"     VALUE(MSG_OBJKY_TO) LIKE  NAST-OBJKY DEFAULT ' '
*"  TABLES
*"      TAB_XNAST STRUCTURE  VNAST
*"      TAB_YNAST STRUCTURE  NAST
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

  REFRESH: tab_xnast,
           tab_ynast.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Beyond Technology: Humane-Led Business Transformation in SAP Project Management
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's complex enterprise landscape, SAP implementations and transformations represent some of the most significant investments an organization can make. These projects not only touch nearly every aspect of a business's operations but also determine its competitive position for years to come. At the heart of successful SAP initiatives lies an often underappreciated but critically important component: the Project Management Office (PMO).

The SAP PMO serves as the strategic command center for these transformative journeys, orchestrating resources, timelines, methodologies, and stakeholder expectations. As organizations continue to navigate digital transformation challenges, the role of the PMO has evolved from a purely administrative function to a strategic powerhouse that drives business value realization.

This evolution brings us to a new paradigm in SAP project management: Humane led Business Transformation with Data-Driven Platforms. This approach recognizes that while technology and data are essential drivers of transformation, the human element remains central to success. The webinar "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects - The New Normal" highlights the importance of finding new approaches to manage SAP implementations more effectively, and this article will explore how combining human-centered leadership with data-driven insights creates a powerful framework for success.

This article explores the evolving landscape of SAP Project Management Offices—examining their critical functions, best practices, challenges, and how a human-centric, data-driven approach is reshaping the way they operate. We'll conclude with an in-depth look at how KTern.AI is revolutionizing SAP PMOs through AI-driven capabilities that amplify human potential rather than replace it.

The Human Element in SAP Project Management

Shifting from Technology-Centric to Human-Centric Approaches

Traditional approaches to SAP implementations have often prioritized technical considerations over human factors. However, experience has shown that technical excellence alone doesn't guarantee project success. The most sophisticated SAP solution will fail to deliver value if the people meant to use it aren't properly engaged, trained, and motivated to adopt new ways of working.

A humane-led approach to SAP transformation prioritizes:

  1. Stakeholder Engagement: Creating genuine buy-in rather than mandated compliance
  2. Change Management: Supporting people through the psychological journey of transformation
  3. Knowledge Building: Developing internal capabilities that enable self-sufficiency
  4. Cultural Alignment: Ensuring that technical solutions match organizational culture and values
  5. User Experience: Designing processes and interfaces with the human experience in mind

Data-Driven Decision Making: The Essential Complement

While the human element is primary, data provides the compass that guides transformation efforts. In modern SAP project management, PMOs leverage data to:

  • Identify bottlenecks and obstacles before they impact timelines
  • Measure user adoption and engagement in real-time
  • Quantify business impact rather than just technical completion
  • Allocate resources based on value creation potential
  • Make course corrections with confidence

The integration of humane leadership with data-driven decision making creates a powerful synergy—one that balances empathy and analytics, heart and head, people and performance metrics. This is what makes the "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects" approach so compelling—it recognizes that data and humanity are complementary forces rather than competing priorities.

The Modern SAP PMO: Strategic Partner in Business Transformation

Redefining the PMO's Role and Value Proposition

The SAP PMO has evolved dramatically from its origins as a project administration center. In today's environment, the PMO functions as a strategic partner in business transformation:

  1. Business Value Orchestrator: Ensuring that technical implementations translate into measurable business outcomes
  2. Transformation Facilitator: Guiding the organization through complex change
  3. Cross-Functional Connector: Breaking down silos between IT and business units
  4. Innovation Catalyst: Identifying opportunities to leverage SAP capabilities for competitive advantage
  5. Knowledge Hub: Building institutional expertise that drives self-sufficiency

The modern PMO is no longer just the project police—it's the transformation enabler that makes success not just possible but probable.

Key Functions of the Modern SAP PMO

The scope of SAP PMO responsibilities continues to expand, driven by the increasing complexity of SAP solutions and the accelerating pace of business change:

  • Strategic Alignment: Ensuring SAP initiatives support broader business objectives
  • Governance and Methodology: Establishing frameworks that balance structure with agility
  • Resource Orchestration: Optimizing the deployment of specialized skills
  • Change Management: Guiding the human side of transformation
  • Value Tracking: Measuring and communicating business benefits realized
  • Knowledge Management: Capturing lessons learned and building organizational capability
  • Vendor Coordination: Managing the ecosystem of implementation partners

Critical Success Factors for Humane-Led, Data-Driven SAP PMOs

Executive Sponsorship and Human-Centric Governance

Successful SAP PMOs operate with strong executive sponsorship that embraces the human element of transformation. Without leaders who prioritize people alongside technology, projects may be completed but transformation will fail.

A human-centric governance framework typically includes:

  • Executive steering committees with representatives who understand both technical and human factors
  • Clear roles and responsibilities with accountability for both technical delivery and user adoption
  • Transparent decision-making processes that incorporate stakeholder feedback
  • Regular governance meetings that address both progress metrics and human experience
  • Balanced scorecards that track technical completion, business impact, and user adoption

Integrated Change Management

Change management is no longer an afterthought but is integrated into every aspect of project planning and execution:

  • Stakeholder analysis that identifies impacts and resistance points early
  • Communication strategies tailored to different audience needs and concerns
  • Training approaches that go beyond feature/function to focus on new ways of working
  • Change impact assessments incorporated into technical design decisions
  • Change readiness metrics tracked alongside technical progress indicators

Data-Informed Resource Management

In the new normal of SAP project management, resource management goes beyond spreadsheets and utilization targets:

  • Skills inventories that capture both technical capabilities and soft skills
  • Capacity planning informed by actual performance data rather than theoretical estimates
  • Resource allocation protocols that match team members to roles that leverage their strengths
  • Training and knowledge transfer approaches based on learning style data
  • Work-life balance metrics to prevent burnout and maintain team health

Continuous Learning and Improvement

A distinguishing characteristic of mature SAP PMOs is their commitment to knowledge building and continuous improvement:

  • Systematic capture of lessons learned through project retrospectives
  • Communities of practice that foster knowledge sharing across teams
  • Performance benchmarking against industry standards and previous projects
  • Experimental approaches that encourage innovation in project delivery
  • Feedback loops that incorporate insights from all levels of the organization

Common Challenges in SAP Transformation and the Human-Centric Response

Despite best intentions, SAP implementations often encounter challenges that can derail progress. A human-centered, data-driven approach offers new ways to address these perennial issues:

Stakeholder Alignment and Expectations Management

Stakeholder alignment remains one of the most common points of failure in SAP projects. The humane-led approach addresses this challenge by:

  • Developing stakeholder journey maps that anticipate concerns and questions
  • Creating personalized communication plans based on stakeholder needs and preferences
  • Establishing regular touchpoints calibrated to appropriate frequency and detail level
  • Leveraging data visualization to make complex information accessible to non-technical stakeholders
  • Building consensus through facilitated workshops and collaborative decision frameworks

Technical Complexity and Integration

The technical aspects of SAP implementations present unique challenges that benefit from a blended human-data approach:

  • Using clear, non-technical language to explain complex concepts to business stakeholders
  • Creating visual representations of technical architectures and integration points
  • Balancing standard SAP processes with legitimate business-specific requirements
  • Ensuring testing scenarios reflect actual human workflows, not just technical paths
  • Collecting and analyzing user feedback on technical design decisions

Balancing Speed and Quality with Human Constraints

In today's fast-paced business environment, SAP PMOs face constant pressure to accelerate implementation timelines:

  • Implementing agile approaches adapted for SAP contexts that maintain sustainable pace
  • Prioritizing functionality based on both business value and human adoption readiness
  • Developing testing strategies that ensure quality without overwhelming teams
  • Creating realistic timelines that account for human factors like learning curves and change absorption
  • Monitoring team wellbeing metrics alongside progress indicators

Leveraging KTern.AI for Humane-Led, Data-Driven Transformation

The integration of human-centered leadership with powerful data analytics represents the frontier of SAP PMO evolution. KTern.AI embodies this integration, offering a comprehensive digital workspace that enhances human capabilities rather than replacing them.

Comprehensive Human-Centered Dashboard Ecosystem

KTern.AI's dashboard ecosystem serves the diverse needs of stakeholders at every level, providing the right information in the right format to the right people:

  1. Portfolio-Segment Dashboard: Provides PMO Heads, IT Directors, VPs, and CIOs with strategic visibility into phase-wise completion status, planned versus actual metrics, and segment statistics. This executive-level view ensures alignment between implementation activities and strategic business objectives, helping leaders make informed decisions that balance technical progress with organizational readiness.
  2. Executive Leadership Dashboard: Tailored specifically for VPs and CIOs, this dashboard delivers an executive summary that cuts through complexity to highlight what matters most. Executives don't need all the details—they need clarity on business impact and critical decision points. The KTern.AI executive dashboard delivers precisely this clarity.
  3. Project Dashboard: Project Managers, Testing Managers, and IT Managers gain real-time insights into work item status, resource utilization, and comprehensive tracking of tasks, issues, and test cases. This operational view drives day-to-day excellence through actionable metrics that balance progress tracking with human factors like workload distribution and capacity constraints.
  4. Phase Cockpit: Provides phase-wise project progress visualization that enables strategic oversight throughout the implementation lifecycle. This ensures stakeholders can track progress against key milestones while maintaining awareness of team performance and adoption readiness.
  5. My Workspace: Over 100+ project members benefit from a personalized workspace that centralizes their tasks, issues, test cases, sign-offs, user stories, risks, and CTAs. This human-centered design recognizes that people perform best when they have clarity on their specific responsibilities within the broader context of the project.

 

Advanced Analytics for Informed Decision Making

KTern.AI transforms raw project data into actionable insights that support human decision-making:

  1. Process Analytics: Project Managers, Testing Managers, and IT Managers gain visibility into process owners, process levels, and Line of Business status. These insights enable process optimization without losing sight of the human impact of process changes.
  2. Quality Assurance Analytics: Testing Managers and PMO Heads can monitor total test cases, overdue work items, defects, and resource utilization. This comprehensive view helps ensure quality outcomes while balancing workload across team members to prevent burnout.
  3. Project Pulse: PMO Heads and IT Directors can track key metrics including project start and end dates, along with progress percentages based on task count, weightage, and milestones. This holistic view of project health enables proactive management of both technical progress and team wellbeing.
  4. Milestone Statistics: PMO Heads gain in-depth analysis of all project milestones, facilitating strategic planning while ensuring that critical path activities receive appropriate attention and resources without overwhelming teams.
  5. Tasks-Insights: Project Leads gain phase-wise insights for each project member, enabling focused leadership and informed resource allocation decisions. This granular visibility supports effective team management and workload balancing.

Collaborative Tools that Enhance Human Interaction

KTern.AI enhances rather than replaces human collaboration through tools designed to make teamwork more effective:

  1. Signoff-Summary View: Project stakeholders gain visibility into parent and child signoffs through an intuitive pyramid view. This clarity around approval status and stakeholder responsibilities optimizes decision-making while ensuring accountability is distributed appropriately.
  2. Auto-Generated Reports: The platform automatically generates meeting reports, CTA tracking, issue reports, and comprehensive workitem documentation. This automation reduces administrative burden while improving communication transparency—a key aspect of human-centered project management.
  3. Event Insights: PMO Heads and IT Directors gain visibility into session details and attendance percentages, maximizing the impact of project events and ensuring effective use of collaborative time. Well-managed meetings create value rather than consuming it.
  4. Portfolio Groups: The platform offers customizable portfolio views that foster seamless collaboration across all project roles while maintaining strategic alignment for executive stakeholders. This flexibility allows teams to organize information in ways that best support their specific needs and working styles.

Transformative Impact on SAP PMO Capabilities

By implementing KTern.AI as part of a humane-led, data-driven approach, organizations can transform their SAP PMO capabilities in several key ways:

  1. Enhanced Visibility with Human Context: The platform provides unprecedented visibility into all aspects of SAP implementations while maintaining awareness of the human factors that influence success. This enables more compassionate and effective leadership throughout the transformation journey.
  2. Accelerated Execution with Sustainable Pace: Streamlined workflows and automated reporting reduce administrative overhead, enabling teams to focus on value-adding activities without creating unsustainable workloads or burnout conditions.
  3. Improved Quality Through Balanced Oversight: Specialized analytics for quality assurance help identify potential issues early while ensuring that quality gates are met consistently—without creating a culture of micromanagement or excessive pressure.
  4. Optimized Resource Utilization with Wellbeing Focus: Detailed insights into resource allocation enable more effective capacity planning while helping leaders ensure that specialized SAP resources are neither underutilized nor overwhelmed.
  5. Better Stakeholder Alignment Through Transparent Communication: Customizable dashboards and automated reports ensure that all stakeholders remain informed and aligned throughout the implementation journey, creating the transparency that is essential for trust-based collaboration.
  6. Knowledge Building for Long-Term Success: By centralizing project information and providing structured repositories for documentation and insights, KTern.AI helps organizations build institutional knowledge that benefits both current implementations and future initiatives.
  7. Data-Informed Decisions with Human Wisdom: The platform's analytics capabilities transform raw project data into actionable insights without removing human judgment from the equation—creating a balanced decision-making approach that is critical to the "new normal" of SAP project management.

Real-World Impact: Humane-Led, Data-Driven Transformation in Action

Organizations that have embraced the humane-led, data-driven approach to SAP transformations with KTern.AI report significant improvements across multiple dimensions:

  • Accelerated Time-to-Value: Implementation timelines reduced by 20-30% through improved coordination and proactive issue resolution
  • Higher User Adoption Rates: 40-50% increase in user adoption during initial go-live phases due to better change management and stakeholder engagement
  • Resource Optimization: 25% improvement in resource utilization through better capacity planning and workload balancing
  • Quality Improvements: 35% reduction in critical defects through enhanced testing coordination and quality analytics
  • Team Satisfaction: Significant improvements in project team satisfaction and reduced burnout, leading to better retention of SAP talent

As highlighted in discussions about "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects," the combination of human-centered leadership with data-driven insights doesn't just change project management metrics—it transforms how teams experience the implementation journey.

The Webinar Connection: Insights on "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects - The New Normal"

The webinar "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects - The New Normal" featuring Jan Musil explores several themes that align with the humane-led, data-driven approach facilitated by KTern.AI:

  1. Reimagining Project Delivery: The webinar discusses how traditional approaches to SAP project management are being challenged by new methodologies that place greater emphasis on human factors alongside technical considerations.
  2. The Importance of Data: A key theme is how data-driven insights can help project leaders make more informed decisions while still maintaining focus on the people who will ultimately determine project success.
  3. Adaptability and Resilience: The discussion highlights how the "new normal" of SAP project management requires greater adaptability and resilience—qualities that are enhanced when teams have both reliable data and human-centered leadership.
  4. Technology as Enabler: Rather than positioning technology as the driver of transformation, the webinar positions digital tools like KTern.AI as enablers that amplify human potential and create space for more strategic thinking.

These insights from the webinar reinforce the value proposition of KTern.AI as a platform that supports the integration of human wisdom with data-driven decision making.

Conclusion: The Human Future of Data-Driven SAP Transformation

As organizations continue to invest in SAP transformations to drive digital innovation and competitive advantage, the most successful will be those that embrace what might truly be called "the new normal"—an approach that puts people at the center while leveraging the power of data and technology to amplify human potential.

The future of SAP PMO excellence lies in this thoughtful integration of human wisdom and digital capabilities—combining the empathy, creativity, and adaptability of experienced professionals with the data processing power, analytical capabilities, and process automation of platforms like KTern.AI.

Organizations that achieve this integration will not only deliver more successful SAP implementations but will also build more resilient, capable teams and more adaptive cultures. In an increasingly complex and fast-changing business environment, this humane-led, data-driven approach to transformation may be the most significant competitive advantage an organization can develop.

By leveraging KTern.AI's comprehensive capabilities within a human-centered transformation philosophy, SAP PMOs can transcend their traditional role as project administrators and emerge as true catalysts for business evolution—driving not just successful implementations but meaningful human progress and sustainable business success.

The technology will continue to evolve. But the fundamental principle remains constant: successful transformation happens when people are empowered by data, not replaced by it. KTern.AI embodies this principle, offering a path to SAP transformation that is both more humane and more effective.

entry.title: 🚀 SAP AI QuickLaunch Series / 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Agent 開発 🚀 - Part 1 Env Setup / 環境構築
entry.content:
Generative AI has leapt from research papers to daily business reality— and SAP is surfing that wave at full speed. In this hands‑on series, I’ll show you how to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes, then grow it into a production‑ready asset—without drowning in theory.

生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っています。このブログシリーズでは、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI エージェントへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

 

📖What You’ll Learn in This Series / 本シリーズで学べること

  • How to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes
    SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI エージェントを “秒速” で動かす方法

  • Hands‑on with LangChain, Google Search Tool, RAG, and Streamlit
    LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAG・Streamlit を使った実装

  • Exposing the agent as a REST API and rebuilding the UI in SAPUI5/Fiori
    エージェントを REST API 化し、UI を SAPUI5/Fiori に載せ替える手順

Time Commitment / 学習時間
Each part is designed to be completed in 10–15 minutes.
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ Series Roadmap / 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 Prologue / プロローグ 
  • Part 1 Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad / 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad [current blog / 現在のブログ]
  • Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain / LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agent Tools: Integrating Google Search / Agentツール: Google 検索を統合
  • Part 4 RAG Basics ①: File Upload & HANA Cloud VectorEngine / RAG 基礎 ①: ファイルアップロード & HANA Cloud ベクターエンジン
  • Part 5 RAG Basics ②: Retriever Tool / RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
  • Part 6 Streamlit UI Prototype / Streamlit でチャットUI
  • Part 7 Expose as a REST API / AI Agent を REST API 化
  • Part 8 Rebuild the UI with SAPUI5 / SAPUI5 でチャットUI を再構築

Note / 注記
Subsequent blogs will be published soon.
続編は順次公開予定です。

Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad
環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad

1 | Overview / 概要

In this chapter, we’ll connect to SAP AI Launchpad using the service key created for the SAP AI Core instance. Then, we’ll proceed to deploy the LLM for running a chat model.

本章では、SAP AI Core インスタンスに対して作成したサービスキーを使い、SAP AI Launchpad に接続します。その後、チャット用 LLM を動かすためのデプロイメントを実施します。

 

2 | Prerequisites / 事前準備

  • BTP sub-account / BTP サブアカウント
  • SAP AI Core instance / SAP AI Core インスタンス
  • SAP AI LaunchPad Subscription / SAP AI Launchpadのサブスクリプション
  • Python 3.13 and pip / Python 3.13環境 & pip
  • VSCode, BAS or any IDE / VSCodeやBASなどのIDE

 

3 | Step 1 – Create a Service Key / Service Key の作成

Creating a service key gives your local scripts a secure, OAuth‑based passport into SAP AI Core. Without it, Python SDK calls have no way to authenticate or discover endpoints.

サービスキーは、ローカルのスクリプトが SAP AI Core に安全かつ OAuth ベースでアクセスするための “パスポート” です。これが無いと、Python SDK はエンドポイントや認証情報を取得できません。

  1. Open your AI Core instance in BTP Cockpit → Instances & Subscriptions.
    BTP Cockpit の Instances & Subscriptions で AI Core インスタンスを開きます。

  2. Go to the Service Keys tab and click Create. Give it any name.
    Service Keys タブで Create をクリックし、任意の名前を入力して作成します。

  3. Download the generated JSON; you’ll need it later.
    生成された JSON をダウンロードして保管します。

NOTE – Resource Group
When calling SAP AI Core from Python you must set an extra environment variable named RESOURCE_GROUP in addition to the values contained in the service key. In this tutorial we’ll simply point it to the built‑in default group.
Python から呼び出す際にはサービスキーに含まれている変数以外に Resource Group と呼ばれる環境変数が必要ですが、本チュートリアルでは既定の default を指定します。

 

4 | Step 2 – Connect AI Launchpad / AI Launchpad との接続

You can deploy an LLM (Large Language Model) directly to SAP AI Core using the service key you created earlier. However, in this guide, we'll use SAP AI Launchpad to make the deployment process easier.
To begin, you'll need to establish a secure connection route from SAP AI Launchpad to SAP AI Core.

先ほど作成した SAP AI Core のサービスキーを使えば、SAP AI Core に直接アクセスして LLM（大規模言語モデル）をデプロイすることも可能です。ただし、ここではより簡単な方法として「SAP AI Launchpad」を使ってデプロイを行います。まずは、SAP AI Launchpad から SAP AI Core へ安全に接続するための経路を準備しましょう。

  1. From Subscriptions open AI Launchpad → Go to Application.
    Subscriptions から AI Launchpad → Go to Application で起動します。

  2. Click Add (top‑right) → API Connection and upload the Service Key JSON.
    右上の Add → API Connection を選び、Service Key JSON をアップロードします。

  3. Select the default Resource Group in the side panel and save.
    サイドパネルで Resource Group に default を選択して保存します。

 

5 | Step 3 – Configure & Deploy an LLM / モデル設定とデプロイ

On the "Configuration" screen of SAP Launchpad, you can select the type of foundation model to use. Think of this as creating a model profile that can be shared across multiple deployments.

Let’s go ahead and create the configuration. In this case, we’ll use the default foundation_models scenario and configure it to use gapt-4o-mini.

SAP Launchpad の「設定」画面では、使用する基盤モデルの種類などを選びます。これは、複数のデプロイメントで共有できる “モデルのプロファイル” のようなものと考えると理解しやすいでしょう。

それでは設定を作成してみましょう。今回は、デフォルトで提供されている foundation_models というシナリオを選択し、gapt-4o-mini を指定します。

  1. Navigate ML Operations → Settings and click Create.
    左メニュー ML 運用 → 設定 で 作成 をクリックします。

  2. Fill in: Name = <any‑name‑you‑like>, Scenario = foundation_models, Version = 0.0.1, Executable = azure-openai.
    設定名 <ご自由に>, シナリオ foundation_models, バージョン 0.0.1, 実行可能 azure-openai を入力します。

  3. On the parameter screen leave modelName = gpt-4o-mini, modelVersion = latest and click Next → Create.
    パラメータ画面では modelName = gpt-4o-mini, modelVersion = latest をそのままに 次へ → 作成。

  4. You’ll land on the new Setting detail page—check that all fields look correct; no extra edits needed.
    作成後に設定詳細の画面へ遷移します。入力内容に誤りがないかざっと確認しましょう（基本的にそのままで OK です）。

  5. Click Create Deployment (top‑right), breeze through the wizard by clicking Next until Create.
    右上の 「デプロイメント作成」 を押し、ウィザードは特に変更せず 「次へ」 → 「作成」 で進めます。

  6. Wait until Status = Running—hit the Refresh icon every few seconds. Copy the Deployment ID shown in the title bar for later use.
    ステータスが 「実行中」 になるまで、更新ボタンで数秒おきにリフレッシュします。タイトルバーに表示される Deployment ID を控えておきましょう。

By the way, there are various LLMs available to run on SAP AI Core. You can browse them in the Generative AI Hub > Model Library section.

ちなみに、SAP AI Core 上で利用可能な LLM はさまざまです。「生成 AI ハブ > モデルライブラリ」画面を開くと、それらの一覧が確認できます。

The Leaderboard also allows you to compare models based on accuracy, token cost, and other metrics.

また、リーダーボード機能では、各モデルの精度やトークンコストなどを比較することもできます。 

 

6 | Next Up / 次回予告

Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain / LangChain でチャットモデルを構築

We’ll finally implement the chat model! Make sure your Python development environment is ready—VS Code or a similar IDE will work perfectly!

Part 2では、いよいよチャットモデルの実装に入ります！VS Code などを使って Python の開発環境を準備しておきましょう！

 

Disclaimer / 免責事項

Disclaimer – All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.
免責事項 – 本ブログに記載された見解および意見はすべて私個人のものであり、私の個人的な立場で発信しています。SAP は本ブログの内容について一切の責任を負いません。

entry.title: Re: Problem to connect Business Application Studio to S/4...
entry.content:
Hello Chris,

entry.title: 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule - (Step #4) Joule Interaction Patterns
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule. Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

 

In this blog, we will dive deep into Interaction Patterns in Joule.

 

Let’s first quickly recap what we learnt so far:

  • Joule is an advanced, generative AI copilot.
  • It is embedded into various SAP applications, for example SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors etc.
  • Users ask their queries in the natively integrated Joule client embedded in SAP cloud solutions, such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors etc. 
  • Joule analyses the prompt, provides a much richer query to the LLM, and finally returns a secure, accurate, and appropriate response back to the user.
  • Technically, Joule is a service running on SAP BTP in a Cloud Foundry environment.
  • Users can access Joule through a diamond icon in the top right-hand corner of the SAP user interface, regardless of the specific system. 
  • Joule uses SAP Cloud Identity Services for authentication and authori

 

What is Interaction Pattern in Joule?

The interaction pattern refers to the different ways users can communicate with Joule to get information, perform tasks, or navigate across applications.

Joule supports 4 types of interaction patterns:

  1. Informational
  2. Navigational
  3. Transactional
  4. Analytical

Below image gives a quick glimpse on these 4 interaction patterns.

 

 

 

Informational

With informational interactions Joule provides knowledge-based results. These are, for example, policy related questions, information in SAP Help, or customer documents stored in third-party repositories.

Joule supports two categories of informational pattern:

  • One is based on SAP owned content with the goal to have all our knowledge-base available via Joule. This is managed by SAP and offered to every Joule user out of the box.
  • Additionally, SAP customers can upload their own content. For example, customers can upload their own HR policies, travel policies, information content, and others. This is called Document Grounding.

 

Example of Informational interaction pattern – in Joule with SuccessFactors

Prompt – “Tell me the birthdays of my team members.”

 

 

 

Prompt - “How many leaves do I have this year?”

 

 

Example of Informational interaction pattern – in Joule with S/4HANA

Prompt: "What is the approval process for purchase orders in our company?"

 

Navigational

In this interaction pattern, Joule interprets if the users want to navigate to a business application and navigate them directly where they want to go.

This is especially helpful for users who are not very familiar navigating SAP applications.

 

Example of navigational interaction pattern – in Joule with SuccessFactors

Prompt – “Show me my organization details”

 

 

Example of navigational interaction pattern – in Joule with S/4HANA

Prompt – “Show me all open sales orders.”.

Joule will show a list of open sales orders. It will also provide a link that redirects the user to the S/4HANA Manage Sales Orders app.

 

Transactional

With transactional pattern, Joule provides users direct entry point to SAP, the backend system, allowing them to do CRUD operation directly from Joule.

For example, review purchase order, create job positions, change address etc.

 

Example of transactional interaction pattern – in Joule with SuccessFactors

Prompt – “Change my address”

 

 

 

 

Analytical

SAP plans to introduce analytical interactions. This will enable analytical interactions based on JustAsk and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Example: “Show me the top five performing products by revenue this quarter."

 

This is in nutshell - Interaction Patterns in Joule!

 

What's Next?

Blog #5 - Joule in SAP SuccessFactors [Will be published soon]

Meanwhile, you may read the blog Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

entry.title: Customer Self Service in SAP for Me - Cloud Purchase Order Edits
entry.content:
Updated on April 22nd, 2025, with new functionality.

We are pleased to announce our new functionality which goes live later on today, which gives you the opportunity to now edit your Purchase Order information for Cloud orders.

From the SAP for Me home page, navigate to Finance and Legal and the Cloud card.

And then go to the Contracts tab and on the right-hand side, Manage PO numbers.

This opens up a Filter page, where you can then see which contracts are available for editing with PO information. 

You can either select one or all of the relevant contracts.

Either enter in a brand new PO number or edit the existing one, and enter a validity date. The validity date option is up to five years out.

Once entered, then click on the Add New PO number and then click on the Confirm button and you will get a confirmation message.

 

You can also enter in that No PO is required.

And then you will get another confirm message to say  that this remains until the end of your contract, unless you chose to edit again.

We created one with "Function Module", but actually it requires a simple "Derivation Rule"

Using the Rule we avoided the error message. After that the BTE 1120 filled the proper values.

Regards

entry.title: Re: RV_MESSAGES_GET inside MM07MFB9_BUCHEN_AUSFUEHREN and...
entry.content:
Hello @Shizofrenik 

What you see is a case of Variable shadowing - XNAST defined in your program that calls RV_MESSAGE_GET is not the same as XNAST defined globally in the V61B function group. RV_MESSAGE_GET is defined in V61B, so all global variables of the function group are visible in all its functions. See SAP Help Validity and Visibility

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: A Step-By-Step Walkthrough for SAP Analytics Cloud compass (PART I)
entry.content:
Hi @ChenNee 

Thank you for your detailed feedback and for encouraging us to submit our suggestions for improvement via the Influence Portal.

Regarding key figure relationships, we are aware that Compass is not intended to uncover mathematical relationships. What we address in our blog post is rather that in real-world customer models, such relationships are often mapped not only via account formulas or calculated key figures, but also via data actions. Or that exception aggregations are sometimes used in the key figures. This creates additional work to make existing models Compass-compatible, or it is currently simply not possible to use them in Compass.

We will continue to work intensively on the new feature and are convinced that it offers great potential, especially in combination with deterministic planning.

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA] What is the use of Technical User(No Addres...
entry.content:
Hello,

To ensure this separation is  effective, a clear technical distinction,such as through User Types is essential. Here’s how it could be defined more precisely,

  1. Classical Users for example Dialog, Service
    These user types should be reserved exclusively for genuine human users who log in interactively or access services. They represent actual individuals or functional users interacting directly with the system.
  2. Technical Users for example System, Communication
    These should be limited to machine-based or system-to-system operations, such as background jobs, APIs, and interface processing. These users do not require dialog logins and operate in non-interactive roles.

Maintain Communication Users App 

Create Communication User 

Let me know if it helps!

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: A Holistic Approach to SAP Security: Introducing the Secure Operations Map (SOM)
entry.content:
What Is the Secure Operations Map—and Why Does It Matter?

The Secure Operations Map is a reference model designed to bring clarity and structure to the broad and often fragmented field of SAP security, helping teams categorize discussions, identify security needs, and map solutions to specific areas of responsibility.

It focuses on operational security—the day-to-day tasks, decisions, and ongoing management needed to run SAP systems securely. While it's interpreted in the context of SAP systems, its structure and principles are also applicable to non-SAP environments.

Secure Operations Map (SOM)

Understanding the five Layers of the Secure Operations Map

The Secure Operations Map breaks down security into five interrelated domains: Environment, System, Application, and Process, supported by the overarching Organization layer.

Environment

This domain covers the non-SAP technical infrastructure that supports SAP solutions.

  • Network Security: Preventing and detecting attacks at the network level using zoning, firewalls, and intrusion detection/prevention.
  • Operating System & Database Security: Enforcing OS and DB-level controls like permissions and encryption to safeguard applications.
  • Client Security: Ensuring end-user devices follow best practices to prevent attacks via compromised clients.

System

This layer focuses on securing the SAP platform itself.

  • Security Hardening: Activating and configuring key security features such as UCON, SAProuter, and frontend hardening.
  • Secure SAP Code: Managing security patches and updates via SAP Security Notes and maintaining a robust patching process.
  • Security Monitoring & Forensics: Combining proactive monitoring with reactive forensics to detect and respond to threats in real time.

Application

Application-level controls are vital in controlling user actions and safeguarding data.

  • User & Identity Management: Handling the full lifecycle of users, including technical and emergency access.
  • Authentication & Single Sign-On: Verifying user identities and enabling seamless, secure access across systems.
  • Roles & Authorizations: Designing and managing authorizations and segregation of duties (SoD) for business roles.
  • Custom Code Security: Managing the entire lifecycle of custom code with secure development and deployment practices.

Process

Security isn’t only technical—it's also about compliance and operational integrity.

  • Regulatory Process Compliance: Implementing controls aligned with laws such as HIPAA, SoX, or Basel III.
  • Data Privacy & Protection: Meeting GDPR and similar legislation requirements with strong confidentiality measures.
  • Audit & Fraud Management: Detecting and preventing fraud while ensuring all controls are auditable and effective.

Organization

This supporting layer provides the strategic and cultural context for everything else.

  • Awareness: Promoting a security-aware mindset throughout the organization.
  • Security Governance: Establishing procedures and responsibilities to support security efforts.
  • Risk Management: Identifying and addressing risks through continuous analysis and mitigation strategies.

Where Should You Start?

With such a broad landscape, one of the first questions is naturally: Where do I begin? The answer isn’t “everywhere”—it’s about security supporting your business, not maximum security possible. Overengineering security can limit functionality and drain resources. Instead, follow this approach:

  • Begin with Baseline Measures: Every SAP system should implement SAP’s baseline security best practices. SAP Note 2253549 provides a solid starting point for that.
  • Perform a Risk-Based Analysis: For critical systems or when you are part of a regulated industry, assess security needs based on specific risks and regulations.
  • Focus on High-Risk Domains First: Identify your most vulnerable or high-impact areas and implement a prioritized improvement plan.
  • Establish Strong Operational Monitoring: Security isn’t static. Put in place continuous monitoring to validate and improve controls over time.

What’s Next?

This post introduces the structure and purpose of the Secure Operations Map. In my next blog post, we’ll dive deeper into each domain, explore common challenges, and show how SAP Security Consulting can support your journey toward a secure, compliant, and resilient SAP landscape.

References: Secure Operations Map

entry.title: Re: What is Capacity Units for SAP Build Code?
entry.content: The capacity units are entitled at the GA level but are not automatically shared across subaccounts. They must be explicitly allocated to each subaccount through quotas. I recommend checking this blog that I found very useful : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-activate-your-free-sap-build-entitlements-with-your-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13736765

entry.title: Supply Chain Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
In 2025, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition ushers in a new age of innovations designed to empower businesses, enhance productivity, and optimize processes. As a vital component of SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP, Supply Chain Management keeps pace with innovation, delivering new capabilities to optimize the flow of goods and services within our customers organizations. In this blog post, I’ll share a selection of the latest innovations from the supply chain area, and how they can help you run your supply chain operations at top performance

Check out the below video by Markus Oertelt for a summary of his top highlights from the 2502 release!

 

 

Order Promising

Display Product Availability Update

The Display Product Availability app is designed to provide business planning and supply chain specialists with real-time information about the availability of products based on multiple criteria such as location, demand, and supply. This intuitive app allows users to quickly view the available quantities of products across various plants, material requirements planning (MRP) areas, and storage locations. It supports the selection of multiple products simultaneously and enables availability checks for any date, starting from today. Users can toggle between the Plant and Storage Location tabs to refine their search based on location-specific data. One of the standout features of this app is its ability to show the carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) footprint of products, helping businesses with SAP Sustainability licenses to track their sustainability goals. Additionally, it displays availability in both the base unit and one alternative unit of measure in parallel, if an alternative unit of measurement is maintained in the master data.

With the 2502 release, the app has been enhanced with new features which show product availability based on a special stock type, such as sales document or customer consignment. A new field has been added for Special Stock type where users can choose between three values. Selecting No Special Stock will display product availability based on the stock and supply and demand documents configured in the scope of availability check. Sales Document displays availability of a specific sales document item and is a useful option when seeking insights for products produced by a make-to-order manufacturing process. Selecting Customer Consignment displays product availability for products allocated as customer consignment stock.

 Transportation Management

With the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Transportation Management has been enhanced with three key features that automate freight document generation and printing, improving efficiency and accuracy. The first new feature allows users to automatically print a loading list or packing list during the checkout of a freight order. This document ensures accurate loading, unloading, and verification of goods, and its automation reduces manual effort while improving communication with carriers. The system generates a loading list for each consignment order, detailing the freight units loaded onto the truck and handed over to the carrier, and sends an automated e-mail notification to the assigned carrier with the loading list attached. Additionally, users can configure extensibility options, allowing for customized e-mail templates and output settings for specific objects like freight orders.

The second feature simplifies communication with freight carriers by introducing an automated e-mail channel for subcontracting or cancelling a freight order or booking. This functionality enables businesses to send e-mails with a PDF attachment containing freight order or booking details, including the waybill and dangerous goods notes. By automating these communications, companies can optimize logistics operations, enhance transparency, and reduce manual workload and costs.

Finally, a new print form for subcontracting freight orders and bookings has been introduced, allowing users to generate and send these forms as e-mail attachments to carriers. This feature supports customization through predefined templates (TM_SUBCONTRACTING_FO, TM_SUBCONTRACTING_FB, TM_TOR_DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATE) and Adobe LiveCycle Designer, ensuring tailored document generation. These enhancements streamline freight management, reduce errors, and improve overall operational efficiency.


Inventory Management

Support for Split Valuations in the Goods Movement Process

 Moving on to inventory where with this release we’ve introduced enhancements to support split valuation of stock, a much-requested feature by our customers which we are excited to roll Split valuation in inventory management refers to the practice of managing and valuing different stocks of the same material in separate categories based on certain criteria, such as different valuation types, origins, or conditions. It allows a company to treat materials of the same type differently depending on factors like their source (e.g., purchased vs. manufactured), quality, or specific use. For example, raw materials may be valued differently than finished goods, or inventory can be split between batches with different costs (e.g., older stock vs. newly purchased stock) or products used as components in the assembly line or sold as standalone components, each with a different price.

 We’ve made numerous enhancements that enable our customers to gain more control over their inventory valuation, enabling better decision-making regarding stock handling, pricing, and reporting. Focusing on split valuation in the goods movement process, the Manage Stock app now supports split-valuation materials, allowing users to view the assigned valuation type for split-valuated materials. It is now simple to then select a particular valuation type and make changes to that stock as required. Similarly, the Transfer Stock - In-Plant has also been enhanced to display materials with different valuation types, enabling you to transfer each type individually within a plant, for example adding x amount of valuation type stock stock y to stock in quality transfer. Also, the Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant app was also enhanced to support different stock valuation types, facilitating transfer of stock postings between plants. 

 Changes made to stock levels as part of goods movement processes will generate a new material document, where the valuation type of stock is also now indicated, alongside the change which was made. Stock monitoring also now reflects different stock valuation types and under Single Material is updated to reflect different valuation types, giving an overview of different stock valuations and displaying the stock history for each type. The Material Documents Overview has also been enhanced, and new columns can be added via the control settings for displaying valuation type and valuation type transfer. Additionally, it offers customization options for storage location control settings and visible columns. Find out more about Split Valuation in Inventory Management in this blog post by Gerhard Welker. 

Click the below image to view a demo of Split Valuations in the Goods Movement Process in action.


 

Schedule Jobs for Stock Data Through the Stock – Multiple Materials App


Through the Stock – Multiple Materials app (App ID: F1595), users can now schedule the export of stock data based on selected filters, ensuring customized and structured inventory analysis. This feature supports processing large volumes of stock data whilst also incorporating additional details such as batch and special stock types in the data set. This efficiently provides users with an easy way to export material stock reports which can be accessed directly in Microsoft Excel via a link in the SAP Fiori app “Display Inventory Analytics Job Results”, enabling seamless stock analysis.


 

Transition from WebGui to Fiori App

In rolling out the new SAP Fiori app “Material Documents Overview”, with 2502 we ensure a smooth transition for end users by introducing a switch in the existing Display Material Document List app (MB51), allowing them to access the new Fiori app more easily. As a means of introducing the new app, users will notice an additional pop-up providing details of the new app and how it can benefit them, whilst giving them flexibility to choose to continue working with the existing app or provide feedback on the new app via a questionnaire.

 

Goods Issue for Production And Maintenance Orders and Stock Transfer Reservations

With the Manage Reservation Items App (F5601), users can more efficiently manage the transfer and movements of goods in their systems by posting a goods movement directly from a reservation. The app enables users to create manual reservations for good movements, search and display details of existing reservation items, or edit, mark as deleted or copy the items of an existing reservation. With the 2502 release, enhancements have been made to the app supporting processes in the areas of production and maintenance management. Further reservation categories for post goods issues are now supported, enabling the post goods issue for a production or maintenance order directly from a reservation. Users can now post goods issue for:

  • Production Order Reservations & Process Order Reservations,
  • Maintenance Order Reservations
  • Manual Stock Transfer Reservations, Material Requirements Planning stock transfer reservations 

 

Batch Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502

With the latest release, new self service configuration objects or configuration items have been added which enable users to specify the message type for system messages (LB 045 and XU 040) and easily switch between error and warning messages. This enhancement improves execution, aligns processes with specific requirements, and boosts usability. Through the Self-Service Configuration UI (SSCUI), users can access new configuration items and make adjustments to existing ones, streamlining Batch Management settings.

Also with the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the Batch Derivation Strategy Type restriction has been replaced by two new, more specific restriction types: Batch Derivation Strategy Type for Sender (KSCHL_R_DS) and Batch Derivation Strategy Type for Receiver (KSCHL_R_DR). These new restriction types allow for separate control of access for the sender and receiver of a batch derivation, enhancing granularity and authorization management. The previous KSCHL_R restriction type, which applied the same control for both sender and receiver, is now deprecated. As of the 2508 release, the KSCHL_R restriction type will be removed from standard business catalogs, and users must ensure that new restrictions are properly configured to avoid loss of access to batch management functionality. This change aims to provide more flexibility in managing batch derivation strategies based on the user’s role and responsibilities.

 

Serial Number Management Enhancements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502

The Serial Number History app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud provides a detailed overview of a material’s lifecycle by tracking serial number movements across various business processes. With the 2502, the app has been enhanced with new filters, detailed views and an improved action log. A new Display Change Log button has been introduced, which can be used to track any changes to system status, unit of measure, material number and further criteria. Users benefit from improved data consistency and a clearer view of any serial number status changes.

On top of this, a new cloud Business ad-in (BAdI) has been introduced to validate serial numbers during business processes. Users can implement custom logic and checks to enforce specific serial number formats and trigger custom error messages when entries do not meet predefined criteria. This enhances data consistency across systems, supports extensibility with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP environment, and maintains system integrity without modifying SAP software code.

The 2502 release introduces new configuration options for managing Serial Number Management settings. This enhancement allows users to define serial number attributes for individual movement types. Instead of relying on a general serial number profile, businesses can now apply specific attributes for different movement types, ensuring greater accuracy in inventory control. This provides greater flexibility and precision in goods movement processes and streamlines operations with detailed tracking requirements.

 

Warehouse Management

For a comprehensive overview of all new innovations in this area, make sure to check out the 2502 blog by Christian Ries. Below, I’ll briefly touch on a few of the highlights.

 Split of Outbound Delivery Orders When Loading Completed

 
This feature enables you to split outbound delivery orders when using Warehouse Management (WM) with Transportation Management (TM). For outbound delivery orders for which you have performed transportation planning, you can now trigger a delivery split as part of the final loading process. When the status “Loading Completed” is set for a freight order, all handling units that have not yet been loaded are split into a new outbound delivery order. This feature enables users to make real-time adjustments to transportation planning during the loading process. For example, if handling units no longer fit on a goods transfer vehicle, they can be split into a new outbound delivery order, ensuring smooth and efficient operations. Once the status "Loading Completed" is set for a freight order (for example, through the "Load or Unload Freight Orders" app or the RF transaction "Loading by HU"), the system automatically splits all handling units that haven’t yet been loaded into a new outbound delivery order. This ensures that the freight order is updated accordingly, enabling accurate tracking and managing of goods throughout the loading process.

 

Scheduling Inventory Document Creation: Cycle Counting

 With 2502 the new Fiori App “Schedule Inventory Document Creation -  Cycle Counting” is introduced which supports the scheduling of the creation of physical inventory documents for cycle counting. It streamlines the cycle counting process by allowing users to schedule the automatic creation of physical inventory documents based on product-related, location-related, and physical inventory parameters. Users can specify criteria such as activity area, cycle counting indicator, physical inventory reason, and document printing preferences to tailor the document creation process.

Automating this recurring inventory task eliminates the need for manual document creation, reducing workload and ensuring consistency with minimal user intervention

 

Lean Customer Returns with Warehouse Management

Customer returns processes have been long supported in Warehouse Management via scope item BKP, which ensures comprehensive and detailed control over return logistics, inspections and refunds. With 2502, we have enhanced returns capabilities in Warehouse Management to also support more streamlined customer returns via scope item BDD. New delivery type LR allows the return of items via this leaner process, avoiding the need for an intermediate step in inventory management and saving time and processing effort. Support of this leaner process will suit users handling high-volume, low value returns who emphasize efficiency in their warehouse management processes.

Click the below image to view a demo of Lean Customer Returns in Warehouse Management,

 

Integration Enhancements with 2502

With 2502, a new API has been introduced for warehouse order events. This enables event-driven automation in Warehouse Management by allowing users to subscribe to events triggered by warehouse task actions, such as creation, confirmation, cancellation, and activation. This enhances operational efficiency and ensures seamless warehouse task management.

On top of this, the Warehouse Order and Task API, the inbound service used to integrate external applications with warehouse management, has been updated to support control cycles. Users can now create, read, update and delete warehouse management control cycles with their source and destination data.




Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!    

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience – anywhere, anytime.   

   

 

Find All of Our Assets    

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Where can I learn how to execute and configure order processing in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring Cross-Application Processes in SAP S/4HANA Sales and Procurement learning journey.

Overview
The learning journey "Configuring Cross-Application Processes in SAP S/4HANA Sales and Procurement" will introduce you to the features and functionality provided for selected cross-application business processes in SAP S/4HANA Sales and Procurement. You will also learn about the associated configuration settings for the selected cross-application business processes. The processes discussed are:

  • Third-Party Order Processing
  • Intercompany Sales Processing
  • Intra- and Cross-Company Stock Transfers
  • Subcontracting
  • Returns Processing

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, participants will be able to:

  • Execute and configure third-party order processing in SAP S/4HANA
  • Execute and configure intercompany sales processing in SAP S/4HANA
  • Execute and configure intra- and cross-company stock transfers in SAP S/4HANA
  • Execute and configure subcontracting in SAP S/4HANA
  • Execute and configure returns processing in SAP S/4HANA

Prerequisites

  • Exploring the Basics of External Procurement in SAP S/4HANA
  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Sales (LJ)
  • Recommended:
    Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sales

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Multi Level Advanced Intercompany Sales information SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public
entry.content:
Just Found Bernd's comment on the advanced intercompany blog from march 2025.

Hi,

Multistage (Multilevel) intercompany sales and multistage intercompany stock transfer are available on SAP’s external roadmap:
Multistage intercompany sales
Multistage intercompany stock transfer

Current plan is to deliver both with S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 (Q1 2026). 
There won’t be any downport option to S/4HANA 2022 or S/4HANA 2023.

The first delivered version of multistage will cover similar scenarios that are covered by advanced, means:

  • Sell-from-stock items are supported
  • Batch split is supported
  • Make-to-Order (with or without variant configuration) is not supported
  • Engineer-to-Order is not supported
  • Sales BOMs are not supported
  • aATP creating subitems is not supported
  • Non-valuated materials / non-stock materials / service materials are not supported

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: Improved Authorization Concept for Business Rules with 1H 2025 Release
entry.content:
Motivation and Transition to Enhanced Authorization

Managing permissions effectively within business rules is increasingly important for our customers. To address their customer feedback and enhance control over business rules , we are launching an upgrade to our authorization concept for working with business rules with  1H 2025.

Previously, the "Metadata Framework → Configure Business Rules" permission was too broad, making it impossible to differentiate between viewing and editing rules, or restricting access to rules for specific applications, such as time management.

Leveraging Role-Based Permissions (RBP), the new concept provides the necessary granularity and ensures users gain precise access, allowing them to manage rules only within their authorized business areas. This enhances security and operational efficiency. Having said that, let's look into the details.

 

How does it work? - Setting up the correct configuration to use RBP for the business rules object

When working with a secured business rule object, it's essential to ensure proper maintenance and configuration.

The following steps are needed to secure your business rule object effectively and ensure system integrity.

Maintain the Security Settings of the Business Rule Object

To secure the business rule object, you must maintain its "Security" section within the Configure Object Definition.

Step 1: Ensure that you apply the following MANDATORY configurations:

  • Secured: Set this value to "Yes" to ensure the business rule object is protected.
  • CREATE Respects Target Criteria: Set this value to "YES", too. This is requested by MDF to ensure that the specified target criteria will be respected in a create operation as well. 

Step 2: Optional, but recommended → Assigning a Permission Category

  • Permission Category: Select the "Business Rules Object Permissions" value. This makes it easier to find the business rule object in the “Manage Permission Roles” page.
  • If you do not select a permission category, the business rule object is added to the “Miscellaneous Permissions” category by default. From a functional perspective, this doesn’t make any difference. However, we recommend using the “Business Rules Object Permissions” category for more efficiency.

If the business rule object is correctly secured, the necessary permissions need to be granted. For existing customers, this means that the permission roles need to be updated. You can find the permission roles that already grant permissions to work with business rules by using our new Check Tool check. I’ll describe that a bit later.

Grant Permissions to Work With Business Rules

When the business rule object is secured, it is essential to grant the appropriate permissions to users. Two mandatory (and one optional) permissions are required, based on the intended level of access for users.

Mandatory Permissions

  • Administrator Permissions → Metadata Framework → "Configure Business Rules": This foundational permission must continue to be granted, as it was required prior to implementing the  new concept.
  • Administrator Permissions → Business Rules Object Permissions (or User Permission → Miscellaneous Permissions): These new permissions must be correctly configured to provide View or Edit access.  If you didn't follow our initial recommendation regarding the permission category, these are placed in the miscellaneous permission category.
     

ATTENTION! Understanding the Differences

Unlike other MDF objects, the Business Rules Object permissions deviate from standard time-dependent object permission behaviors. Though specific, granular actions can be assigned to time-dependent MDF objects, these are not applicable for a configuration object such as the business rule object; hence, we simplify permissions by distinguishing between only View and Edit.

  • View Permissions:
    Ensure both view actions, View Current and View History, are selected for roles meant to have just View access.
  • Edit Permissions:
    Since Edit encompasses View, both view permissions must be flagged alongside all edit actions: Create, Insert, Correct, Delete. Should any action be missing, such as forgetting to flag View History, the system will not grant view access. Rest assured, the Check Tool will highlight these misconfigurations, which I will detail shortly.

Optional Permissions

To fine-tune authorization further, you may apply restrictions by setting Target Criteria in the Role Assignment. This allows specifying access to particular business areas, like granting Onboarding Business Admin permissions by selecting all Onboarding Scenarios.

What to expect? - What will be impacted by the new authorizations?

When the business rule object is secured and configured with Role-Based Permissions, it significantly impacts various areas in the system.

Let’s dig into the details.

Business Rules Admin Page (List of Business Rules)

The Business Rules Admin page is the central entry point to work with business rules.

It provides a list of all rules that exist in the system. If the business rule object is secured, it can only be accessed if the user has the Configure Business Rules permission + (at least) View permission for the Business Rule object.

Unlike view and edit permissions, certain functionalities are not offered when only the view permission is granted. This includes:

  • The “Create New Rule” button
  • The “Delete” button

If Edit & View or just View permissions are granted and, additionally, target criteria are restricted to certain scenarios, then:

  • The result list will only show rules based on these allowed scenarios.
  • If the user selects an unauthorized scenario via the Scenario filter in the filter bar, the result list will not show any rule for that scenario, even if some exist.
  • Instead, the user will see a message that no data is available, or the necessary permission is missing.

Configure Business Rules page

Also, the Configure Business Rule page doesn’t offer certain functionalities if the user has view permissions only, which includes:

  • Change Scenario: As changing the scenario changes the rule configuration, it is not offered.
  • Insert New Record & Create New Rule: Neither button appears on the UI.
  • Take Action menu with the actions Make Correction, Copy Rule & Permanently Delete Entry: These actions are covered by the edit permissions and not offered when only View is granted.

The only available action in the Take Action Menu that might be available if the user has just view permission is the Add to Transport Bundle action. This is a separate permission and not covered by the business rule object permissions at all.

Rule Assignment in Application Configuration

Efficient rule assignment is facilitated within most of our applications UIs, offering intuitive ways to manage and engage with business rules.

Several UIs, such as the Manage Business Configuration UI in the screenshot below, feature options to create new rules (“+” icon) or view assigned rules (“Quick Card” icon) through a streamlined link to the Business Rules Framework, ensuring users have immediate access.

With the new authorization concept, these UIs react according to the granted permissions of the users.

The application UIs also react to the defined target criteria. For assigning rules, the field value help offers the list of rules that are allowed to be assigned, based on the rule scenario. If the user's role hasn’t been granted access to the expected scenario, the drop down list to select a rule will be empty.

As promised - Check Tool Check for Adapting RBP roles

For existing customers, deciding to add a new security level to Business Rules comes along with the need to find, review, and potentially adjust permission roles that already exist in the system.

To support that effort, we provide a new Check Tool check, designed to scan all existing roles, and find out the ones that might have been designed before for working with business rules before.

Here's an in-depth look at this 2-stepped check:

As I mentioned in the very beginning, the new authorization concept comes with 2 main configuration prerequisites: First, securing the business rule object itself and second, granting the corresponding permissions. This is also reflected in a 2-stepped approach in the Check Tool check.

Step 1 verifies the security setup of the business rules object within the system:

  • The check inspects the security section of the business rules object in "Configure Object Definition", to ensure the mandatory settings are correctly maintained.
    • Secured = yes
    • CREATE respects target criteria = yes
  • If the business rule object's configuration is incomplete, the assessment stops here, and the result list points out the incomplete configuration.
  • The Proposed Solution section of the check offers guidance on how the business rule object configuration needs to be adjusted to meet the required security settings, allowing the process to continue.

Once the initial configuration is verified, the tool proceeds with Step 2 to examine all permission roles associated with business rules maintenance permissions:

  • The check evaluates ALL roles, so expect it to run for quite a while 😉, for one of the following criteria:
    • Administrator Permission Metadata Permissions → Configured Business Rules: is flagged AND/OR
    • Administrator Permission → Business Rule Object Permission: at least one of the actions on the business rule object is flagged
  • The Check Tool generates an exhaustive Check Result list of all roles containing any kind of inconsistency in the expected permission configurations regarding business rules.
  • Each entry in the result includes:
    • Permission Role Name[1]: Linked directly to the Manage Permission Roles page for an easy access to review and adapt the role.
    • Identified Issues: Specific details about what inconsistency was detected for this role.
    • Role Status: Current operational status of the role in question (active/inactive).

If you are familiar with the Check Tool, you’ll probably know that some checks are designed to offer a so called “Quick Fix”. We thought about offering such a quick fix in case the check finds roles that violate the expected configuration. However, that would have meant providing all roles with the comprehensive permission to view and edit all business rules again, because we have no way of knowing how a customer has designed the role concept.

We decided against providing such a Quick Fix, as we think the decision to enable the security for the business rule object and benefit from the improved authorization options it offers should be an active decision taken by the customer

If you decide to secure the rules object and implement an enhanced permission concept for your company, you need to review your existing roles and decide which permissions these roles should be granted in the future.

Anything more? - Additional Details to Enhance Your Understanding

  1. Execution of Business Rules: What I’d like to point out clearly is that the execution of business rules is NOT AFFECTED, neither by securing the business rule object nor by (mis-) configuring any permission role. Executing rules is independent from the permission to view or maintain rules!
  2. Limitation – Field-level Overrides: For a configuration object, such as the Business Rule object, defining field-level overrides doesn’t make sense and is NOT SUPPORTED. If anything is configured for field-level overrides, as in the screenshot below, it’s ignored by the system.

Finally, I hope you agree that the new authorization concept for business rules provides the needed granularity and ensures that users can view or manage rules strictly within their authorized business areas. This enhances both security and operational efficiency.

I also hope you agree with our approach that system administrators must manage permissions proactively to align with the new setup. Proper configuration prevents unauthorized access and this needs to fit the very own customer permission role concept.

The introduction of a new check in the Check Tool is our support to review and adjust permission roles effectively to make the transition as smooth as possible.

And, if there are any open questions, please feel free to comment on this blog. I’d be happy to provide answers. Meanwhile, please check out our What’s New Viewer as well as our enhanced documentation (Permissions for Business Rules, Check Tool check ).

 

[1] To effectively utilize the "Manage Permission Roles" UI, users must have RBP Admin View access as a minimum. To actively modify permission roles, RBP Admin Edit permission is required. (Admin Center → “Manage Role-Based Permission Access.”)

/SCWM/IF_API_WHR_OUTBOUND method: SET_HEADER_STATUS ( Since you are added status in header so need to change value into header status)

In that link, SAP is already provided the codes with example  for EWM outbound delivery and use that method SET_HEADER_STATUS to set value activate or deactivate.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Who offers expert health and safety assignment help f...
entry.content:
Hello @CMIA 

 

I believe you're located in the UK? If that's the case start with the The Health and Safety Executive (HSE: Information about health and safety at work). This is the regulating body for this subject. There you'll find a trove of information, both legal and "best practices". 

In a similar fashion, consult both EU OSHA and other "OSHAS" like the Canadian or the Australian - as things are in turmoil over the USA, you should maybe avoid sources there.

Some workers unions and industry unions sites provide useful information about HSE. 

Hope this helps

Paulo Pinto

entry.title: Error during compilation of odata proxy
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm following the course https://learning.sap.com/courses/building-apps-with-the-abap-restful-application-programming-model and I'm stuck in week 5, unit 2.

I keep getting the error 

"Error during compilation of '$OPROXY_SRVD#ZSC_RAP_AGENCY_1848'."

The error is thrown from the following code:

    DATA service_consumption_name TYPE cl_web_odata_client_factory=>ty_service_definition_name.

    DATA(http_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_url( i_url = 'https://sapes5.sapdevcenter.com' ).
    http_client = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( i_destination = http_destination ).

    service_consumption_name = to_upper( 'ZSC_RAP_AGENCY_1848' ).

    odata_client_proxy = cl_web_odata_client_factory=>create_v2_remote_proxy(
      EXPORTING
        iv_service_definition_name = service_consumption_name
        io_http_client             = http_client
        iv_relative_service_root   = '/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZAGENCYCDS_SRV/' ).

My service consumption model is generated from the metadata on https://sapes5.sapdevcenter.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZAGENCYCDS_SRV/, so everything should fit together.

What could be the problem?

PS: Feel free to look in the code in the Abap Trial Platfrom.

Best Regards,

Martin

entry.title: Re: Why quantities in Chart in SAP IBP Excel Add-In are s...
entry.content:
The reason is that, the chart uses the chart functionality of MS Excel. And there are many settings one can choose in these charts.

In this sense, you will see a quantity 1,582 displayed in full as 1,582 in the chart of Best Practices template DP 140 Global Demand Plan:

However, if we look at the same values, on the same aggregation level (time and Master Data attributes), in the chart of Best Practices template  DP 110 Historical Data Cleanse:

Compare the values above the column charts in the 2 screenshots above.

The reason of the difference is not a difference in the width of the columns in our Excel Sheets, nor is it because of any settings in 'Edit View' > 'View Formats' (which opens the hidden sheet 'IBPFormattingSheet'). Instead, the reason is to be found within the options of the charts in either templates. The relevant 'Chart Design' options can be easily inspected and/or changed, by clicking on the numbers in the chart, selecting them as such:


Subsequently right-click, and choose 'Format Data Label...':

In the first case, we note that the 'Number' > Category' is defined as 'Number' in the following manner:

But if we execute the same process for the second template, we see a different setting:

Right-click on the selected Data Label, and choose 'Format Data Label...':

We notice that the 'Number' > Category' is defined not as 'Number' here but instead as 'Custom' and in the following manner:

[>=1000000]0,,"M";[>=1000]0,"K";#""

And this is the reason for the difference. This is a custom number format used in Excel to display large numbers in a more readable way. It can be understood as such:

  • [>=1000000]0,,"M": If the number is greater than or equal to 1,000,000, it will be displayed in millions (M). For example, 2,500,000 will be shown as 2.5M.
  • [>=1000]0,"K": If the number is greater than or equal to 1,000 but less than 1,000,000, it will be displayed in thousands (K). For example, 1,582 will be shown 2K since the number would be divided by 1,000 and rounded to the nearest whole number.
  • #: This is the default format for numbers that don't meet the previous conditions. It will display the number as is.

I posted this Q&A to be of help to the wider SAP IBP the community. Note that, once understood that the SAP IBP Excel Add-In Chart is using the functionalities of the Microsoft Excel 'Chart Design', one can start to experiment with a whole variety of different chart types, for example by right-clicking on the chart and choosing 'Change Series Chart Type...'.

entry.title: Re: Responsibility Management: Creating custom teams & responsibility definitions in S/4HANA 202
entry.content:
hi @WilliamChi : There is no documentation if this app is deprecated. I suspect 2 things here

1. Your app is referring to a old catalog which is no longer valid. This can happen when you have upgraded from a lower version to a new version.

2. You don't have sufficient authorization to create a new team category, you can check in SU53 trace and see if you are getting authorization error. You can also ask your security team to check by running ST01 authorization trace for your ID.

 

Regards

Anirban
